definition: Identify the scientific factor that induces distribution shift, such as particle pileup level, signal-track curvature, material fidelity level, assay, molecular size, or scaffold. Use distance or shared membership along that factor as the SPECTRA novelty axis.
example_usage: python metadata_distance_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --columns shift_factor

citations.title: GeSS: Benchmarking Geometric Deep Learning under Scientific Applications with Distribution Shifts

evidence.summary: The paper categorizes scientific shifts as conditional, covariate, or concept shifts and studies different levels of OOD information.
The paper tabulates scientific shift cases and says source subgroups are partitioned by the factor undergoing shift.
The paper defines concrete shift factors including pileup, signal curvature, material fidelity, assay, size, and scaffold.

limitations: Requires domain knowledge to identify the causal or nuisance factor being shifted.
Categorical factors need a justified distance if converted to a continuous axis.
Multiple shift factors may interact and should not be collapsed without checking.
quality_gates: Name the shifted probability mechanism or scientific factor.
Report whether the factor is categorical, ordinal, scalar, or graph-valued.
Run separate SPECTRA curves for distinct shift mechanisms when feasible.
